_**User (2026-03-31 02:12:16Z)**_

Create a discord bot that tracks the ranks of players in Teamfight Tactics (aka TFT) and displays them in a leaderboard from highest rank to lowest. The bot should be built using discord.py and use the RIOT Games developer API (available at https://developer.riotgames.com/) to get the players ranks. Players should be able to self register by using the bot by providing their RIOT Games username and tag on the NA server specifically. Include auto-refresh caching to reduce Riot API calls, auto posting to the channel at timed increments, embeds for leaderboard with pagination, leaderboards per discord server, and the ability for users to link their main accounts in other regions for visual purposes if NA isn't their main region.

---

## TFT Rank Tracker Discord Bot — Architecture Plan

---

### 1. File Structure

```
tft-bot-claude/
├── bot.py                   # Entry point: creates Bot, loads cogs, starts
├── config.py                # Central config (env vars, constants, region maps)
├── database.py              # DB connection pool, schema init, all SQL helpers
├── riot_client.py           # Async Riot API wrapper (all HTTP logic lives here)
├── cache.py                 # In-memory TTL cache with per-entry invalidation
│
├── cogs/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registration.py      # /register, /unregister, /link-region, /my-accounts
│   ├── leaderboard.py       # /leaderboard command + PaginatedView class
│   └── admin.py             # /set-channel, /set-interval, /force-refresh
│
├── .env                     # DISCORD_TOKEN, RIOT_API_KEY (not committed)
├── .env.example             # Template for .env
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
```

Keeping it flat: no `utils/` directory unless you hit 8+ cog files. One file per concern, not per class.

---

### 2. Database Schema (SQLite + aiosqlite)

Four tables. The schema lives in `database.py` and runs on startup via `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`.

```sql
-- One row per Discord user per guild. NA is the "primary" account.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS registrations (
    guild_id        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discord_id      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    game_name       TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Riot gameName (NA)
    tag_line        TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Riot tagLine (NA)
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,         -- resolved once on register
    summoner_id     TEXT NOT NULL,         -- NA summoner ID, resolved on register
    PRIMARY KEY (guild_id, discord_id)
);

-- Linked accounts in other regions (0..N per registration row).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS linked_accounts (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    guild_id        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discord_id      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    region          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- "KR", "EUW", "EUNE", "BR", etc.
    game_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
    tag_line        TEXT NOT NULL,
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,
    summoner_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE (guild_id, discord_id, region),
    FOREIGN KEY (guild_id, discord_id) REFERENCES registrations(guild_id, discord_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Rank cache. One row per (guild_id, discord_id) for NA,
-- plus one row per linked_accounts.id for other regions.
-- Keyed by puuid so the same player in two guilds shares nothing (intentional).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rank_cache (
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,
    region          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- "NA", "KR", etc.
    tier            TEXT,                  -- "GRANDMASTER", "DIAMOND", etc. NULL if unranked
    rank            TEXT,                  -- "I", "II", "III", "IV"
    lp              INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    wins            INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    losses          INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    updated_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- Unix timestamp
    PRIMARY KEY (puuid, region)
);

-- Per-guild bot settings.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guild_settings (
    guild_id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    channel_id      INTEGER,               -- where to auto-post leaderboard
    post_interval   INTEGER DEFAULT 3600,  -- seconds between auto-posts
    last_post_at    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     -- Unix timestamp of last auto-post
    last_message_id INTEGER                -- message to edit on next auto-post
);
```

Key decisions:
- `rank_cache` is keyed by `(puuid, region)`, not by `(guild_id, discord_id)`. If the same Riot account appears across two Discord servers (unlikely but possible), they share one cache row. This is intentional — it halves API calls.
- `updated_at` is a Unix integer, not a datetime string. Simpler to compare against `time.time()` in Python.
- `linked_accounts` uses `ON DELETE CASCADE` so unregistering a player clears their linked accounts automatically.
- `guild_settings.last_message_id` enables the edit-vs-post decision for auto-posting.

---

### 3. `config.py` — Region Routing

```python
# Platform hosts (for summoner/league endpoints)
PLATFORM_HOSTS = {
    "NA": "na1.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "kr.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "euw1.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "eun1.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "br1.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "jp1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "la1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "la2.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "oc1.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "tr1.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "ru.api.riotgames.com",
    "SG": "sg2.api.riotgames.com",
    "PH": "ph2.api.riotgames.com",
    "TW": "tw2.api.riotgames.com",
    "VN": "vn2.api.riotgames.com",
    "TH": "th2.api.riotgames.com",
}

# Regional routing hosts (for account-v1 PUUID lookups)
REGIONAL_HOSTS = {
    "NA": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "SG": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "PH": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "TW": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "VN": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "TH": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "sea.api.riotgames.com",
}

TIER_ORDER = [
    "IRON", "BRONZE", "SILVER", "GOLD", "PLATINUM",
    "EMERALD", "DIAMOND", "MASTER", "GRANDMASTER", "CHALLENGER"
]
RANK_ORDER = ["IV", "III", "II", "I"]
CACHE_TTL = 300  # 5 minutes in seconds
PLAYERS_PER_PAGE = 10
```

---

### 4. `riot_client.py` — API Wrapper

A single `RiotClient` class holds one `aiohttp.ClientSession` created in an async context manager. All methods raise a custom `RiotAPIError` subclass with the HTTP status attached — callers catch it and surface a clean message to Discord.

Key methods:

```python
class RiotClient:
    async def get_puuid(self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str) -> str
    async def get_summoner_by_puuid(self, puuid: str, region: str) -> dict
    async def get_tft_rank(self, summoner_id: str, region: str) -> dict | None
    # Returns the RANKED_TFT entry dict, or None if unranked.
    # Filters internally: next(e for e in data if e["queueType"] == "RANKED_TFT", None)

    async def get_full_rank_data(self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str) -> dict
    # Convenience: chains all three calls, returns unified dict.
```

Rate limiting: Riot's development key is 20 req/1s, 100 req/2min. For a small bot (<50 players), this won't be an issue with 5-minute caching. Do not implement a rate limiter for now — handle 429 responses by catching them in `RiotAPIError` and logging. Add a backoff decorator only when you actually hit limits in production.

The session is created once in `bot.py` (`async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session`) and passed into the cog constructors. Do not create sessions per-request.

---

### 5. `cache.py` — In-Memory TTL Layer

The SQLite `rank_cache` table IS the cache (persists across restarts). The in-memory layer is optional but useful for burst deduplication within a single process. Recommended approach: skip a separate in-memory cache. Use the DB directly.

Why: aiosqlite reads from WAL-mode SQLite are fast (microseconds). The TTL check is a single `SELECT updated_at FROM rank_cache WHERE puuid=? AND region=?`. The 5-minute TTL provides sufficient protection against the Riot API rate limit. Adding an in-memory dict creates a second source of truth to keep in sync, which is a bug surface. Keep it simple.

If you later need sub-100ms response to a slash command, add:
```python
# In database.py
_mem_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[dict, float]] = {}
```
...as a module-level dict, with a helper that checks it before hitting SQLite. But do not do this at initial build.

---

### 6. Cog Design

#### `cogs/registration.py`

Commands:
- `/register <game_name> <tag_line>` — Links the invoking Discord user to an NA Riot account for this guild. Resolves puuid + summoner_id on the spot (immediate Riot API call), stores in `registrations`. Fetches and caches initial rank. Ephemeral response.
- `/unregister` — Removes the user's row from `registrations` (CASCADE deletes linked accounts). Ephemeral response.
- `/link-region <region> <game_name> <tag_line>` — Adds a row to `linked_accounts`. Resolves puuid + summoner_id for that region's platform host. Validates that `region` is in `PLATFORM_HOSTS`. Ephemeral response.
- `/unlink-region <region>` — Removes a specific `linked_accounts` row.
- `/my-accounts` — Shows the user's NA account and all linked regions. Ephemeral embed. No rank data, just account names.

Registration flow for `/register`:
1. Call `riot_client.get_puuid(game_name, tag_line, "NA")`
2. Call `riot_client.get_summoner_by_puuid(puuid, "NA")`
3. `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO registrations ...`
4. Call `riot_client.get_tft_rank(summoner_id, "NA")` and write to `rank_cache`
5. Reply with confirmation embed showing current rank

#### `cogs/leaderboard.py`

Commands:
- `/leaderboard` — Fetches all registrations for the guild, pulls rank from `rank_cache` (refreshing stale entries), sorts by rank, renders paginated embed.

The `PaginatedView` class:

```python
class PaginatedView(discord.ui.View):
    def __init__(self, pages: list[discord.Embed], timeout: float = 180):
        ...
    @discord.ui.button(label="◀", style=discord.ButtonStyle.secondary)
    async def prev_page(self, interaction, button): ...
    @discord.ui.button(label="▶", style=discord.ButtonStyle.secondary)
    async def next_page(self, interaction, button): ...
```

Each page is a pre-built `discord.Embed`. Build all pages before showing the view. Disable buttons at boundaries (first page: disable prev; last page: disable next). Update by calling `interaction.response.edit_message(embed=self.pages[self.current], view=self)`.

Rank sorting: convert `(tier, rank, lp)` to a numeric score for `sorted()`:
```python
def rank_score(row) -> int:
    if row["tier"] is None:
        return -1
    tier_val = TIER_ORDER.index(row["tier"]) * 10000
    rank_val = RANK_ORDER.index(row["rank"]) * 400 if row["rank"] else 0
    return tier_val + rank_val + row["lp"]
```

Embed format per player (one line per player in the field):
```
1. PlayerName#TAG — Diamond II 75 LP  |  [KR] GrandmasterRiotID#KR1
```
The linked region data is displayed as a parenthetical suffix. If the linked account's rank cache is stale, show `[KR] ⏳ updating...` and kick off a background task to refresh it.

#### `cogs/admin.py`

Commands:
- `/set-channel <channel>` — Writes `channel_id` to `guild_settings`. Requires `manage_guild` permission check via `@app_commands.default_permissions(manage_guild=True)`.
- `/set-interval <hours>` — Writes `post_interval` (converted to seconds) to `guild_settings`. Minimum 1 hour enforced in code.
- `/force-refresh` — Deletes all `rank_cache` rows for this guild's players and immediately re-fetches. Useful after a patch. Responds with a progress message that gets edited on completion.
- `/force-post` — Manually triggers the auto-post logic for this guild.

---

### 7. Background Task: Cache Refresh + Auto-Posting

In `bot.py`, register one `tasks.loop` task. Do not put it in a cog — lifecycle management is cleaner on the bot object itself.

```python
@tasks.loop(minutes=1)
async def background_loop(bot):
    now = int(time.time())

    # Step 1: Refresh stale rank cache entries
    stale_rows = await db.get_stale_cache_entries(now - CACHE_TTL)
    for row in stale_rows:
        try:
            rank_data = await riot_client.get_tft_rank(row["summoner_id"], row["region"])
            await db.upsert_rank_cache(row["puuid"], row["region"], rank_data, now)
        except RiotAPIError as e:
            log.warning(f"Failed to refresh {row['puuid']}/{row['region']}: {e}")

    # Step 2: Auto-post leaderboards for guilds whose interval has elapsed
    guilds_due = await db.get_guilds_due_for_post(now)
    for guild_settings in guilds_due:
        await post_leaderboard(bot, guild_settings)
        await db.update_last_post(guild_settings["guild_id"], now)
```

The `get_stale_cache_entries` query joins `rank_cache` against both `registrations` and `linked_accounts` so it only refreshes players that are still registered somewhere. This prevents accumulating orphaned cache rows.

```sql
-- get_stale_cache_entries
SELECT rc.puuid, rc.region,
       COALESCE(r.summoner_id, la.summoner_id) as summoner_id
FROM rank_cache rc
LEFT JOIN registrations r   ON r.puuid = rc.puuid AND rc.region = 'NA'
LEFT JOIN linked_accounts la ON la.puuid = rc.puuid AND la.region = rc.region
WHERE rc.updated_at < :cutoff
  AND (r.puuid IS NOT NULL OR la.puuid IS NOT NULL)
```

The loop runs every 1 minute but only acts when TTL or interval conditions are met. This gives you fine-grained auto-post scheduling without needing per-guild async tasks.

---

### 8. Auto-Posting: Edit vs. New Message

Strategy: **always try to edit; fall back to a new post.**

```python
async def post_leaderboard(bot, guild_settings):
    channel = bot.get_channel(guild_settings["channel_id"])
    if channel is None:
        return

    embeds = await build_leaderboard_embeds(guild_settings["guild_id"])
    # Auto-posts always show page 1 only, no pagination buttons
    # (pagination is only for interactive /leaderboard responses)
    embed = embeds[0]
    embed.set_footer(text=f"Page 1/{len(embeds)} • Updated {datetime.utcnow():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M} UTC")

    if guild_settings["last_message_id"]:
        try:
            msg = await channel.fetch_message(guild_settings["last_message_id"])
            await msg.edit(embed=embed)
            return
        except (discord.NotFound, discord.Forbidden):
            pass  # fall through to posting new

    msg = await channel.send(embed=embed)
    await db.update_last_message_id(guild_settings["guild_id"], msg.id)
```

Why page 1 only for auto-posts: auto-posted messages are passive — they sit in a channel as a status board. Interactive pagination belongs to slash command responses which have a 15-minute interaction token window anyway. If the leaderboard has multiple pages, indicate it in the footer. Users who want to paginate use `/leaderboard`.

---

### 9. `bot.py` — Entry Point Structure

```python
class TFTBot(commands.Bot):
    def __init__(self):
        intents = discord.Intents.default()
        # No message_content intent needed — slash commands only
        super().__init__(command_prefix=None, intents=intents)
        self.db: Database = None
        self.riot: RiotClient = None

    async def setup_hook(self):
        self.db = Database("tft.db")
        await self.db.init()  # runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
        self.riot = RiotClient(session=aiohttp.ClientSession(),
                               api_key=os.environ["RIOT_API_KEY"])
        await self.load_extension("cogs.registration")
        await self.load_extension("cogs.leaderboard")
        await self.load_extension("cogs.admin")
        await self.tree.sync()  # sync slash commands globally
        background_loop.start(self)

    async def close(self):
        background_loop.cancel()
        await self.riot.session.close()
        await self.db.close()
        await super().close()
```

`setup_hook` is the correct discord.py 2.x lifecycle method for async initialization. Do not use `on_ready` for setup — it can fire multiple times.

Call `tree.sync()` globally on every startup during development. Once the command list is stable, comment it out (global sync propagates in ~1 hour but isn't rate-limited; guild sync is instant but per-guild).

---

### 10. `requirements.txt`

```
discord.py>=2.3.0
aiosqlite>=0.19.0
aiohttp>=3.9.0
python-dotenv>=1.0.0
```

`.env` file:
```
DISCORD_TOKEN=your_token_here
RIOT_API_KEY=RGAPI-xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

---

### 11. Architectural Decisions Summary

**SQLite over PostgreSQL**: For a single-process bot tracking under a few hundred players, SQLite with WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`) is sufficient and eliminates an external dependency. Switch to PostgreSQL if you need multi-process deployment or hit WAL contention.

**One aiohttp session for all Riot calls**: Sessions are expensive to create. Create one in `setup_hook`, pass it to `RiotClient`, close it in `bot.close()`.

**Cogs receive `db` and `riot` via constructor injection**: The cog `__init__` receives the already-initialized `Database` and `RiotClient` objects from the bot. Avoids globals or `self.bot.db` access patterns that are hard to test.

**No separate in-memory cache**: SQLite WAL reads are fast enough. The DB is the single source of truth. Revisit after profiling if `/leaderboard` response time exceeds 2 seconds.

**puuid as the stable identifier**: Riot IDs (gameName#tagLine) can change. puuid is permanent. Store puuid in all tables; only store the display gameName+tagLine for rendering.

**Per-guild isolation via `guild_id` in every query**: All data is scoped to `guild_id`. The bot can be in 100 servers with no data leakage between them.

**Refresh stale entries proactively in the background**: The 1-minute background loop ensures cache entries are refreshed before users ask for the leaderboard, not on-demand. This means `/leaderboard` responses are nearly instantaneous — they read from cache and only block if an entry is stale AND hasn't been refreshed yet (race condition window: at most 1 minute).

**`/register` resolves and caches immediately**: Registration is the one place where a blocking Riot API call is acceptable — the user just submitted a command and is waiting for confirmation. All subsequent data is served from cache.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/bot.py` — Entry point, bot class, background loop, lifecycle management
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/database.py` — Schema, all SQL helpers, aiosqlite connection management
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/riot_client.py` — All Riot API HTTP logic, region routing, error handling
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/cogs/leaderboard.py` — Rank sorting, embed building, PaginatedView, auto-post render
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/config.py` — Region maps, TIER_ORDER, RANK_ORDER, constants shared across all modules
```

Good. Clean environment, greenfield project. Now I have everything I need to design a complete, practical architecture. Here is the full plan.

## TFT Rank Tracker Discord Bot — Architecture Plan

---

### 1. File Structure

```
tft-bot-claude/
├── bot.py                   # Entry point: creates Bot, loads cogs, starts
├── config.py                # Central config (env vars, constants, region maps)
├── database.py              # DB connection pool, schema init, all SQL helpers
├── riot_client.py           # Async Riot API wrapper (all HTTP logic lives here)
├── cache.py                 # In-memory TTL cache with per-entry invalidation
│
├── cogs/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registration.py      # /register, /unregister, /link-region, /my-accounts
│   ├── leaderboard.py       # /leaderboard command + PaginatedView class
│   └── admin.py             # /set-channel, /set-interval, /force-refresh
│
├── .env                     # DISCORD_TOKEN, RIOT_API_KEY (not committed)
├── .env.example             # Template for .env
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
```

Keeping it flat: no `utils/` directory unless you hit 8+ cog files. One file per concern, not per class.

---

### 2. Database Schema (SQLite + aiosqlite)

Four tables. The schema lives in `database.py` and runs on startup via `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`.

```sql
-- One row per Discord user per guild. NA is the "primary" account.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS registrations (
    guild_id        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discord_id      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    game_name       TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Riot gameName (NA)
    tag_line        TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Riot tagLine (NA)
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,         -- resolved once on register
    summoner_id     TEXT NOT NULL,         -- NA summoner ID, resolved on register
    PRIMARY KEY (guild_id, discord_id)
);

-- Linked accounts in other regions (0..N per registration row).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS linked_accounts (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    guild_id        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discord_id      INTEGER NOT NULL,
    region          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- "KR", "EUW", "EUNE", "BR", etc.
    game_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
    tag_line        TEXT NOT NULL,
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,
    summoner_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE (guild_id, discord_id, region),
    FOREIGN KEY (guild_id, discord_id) REFERENCES registrations(guild_id, discord_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Rank cache. One row per (guild_id, discord_id) for NA,
-- plus one row per linked_accounts.id for other regions.
-- Keyed by puuid so the same player in two guilds shares nothing (intentional).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rank_cache (
    puuid           TEXT NOT NULL,
    region          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- "NA", "KR", etc.
    tier            TEXT,                  -- "GRANDMASTER", "DIAMOND", etc. NULL if unranked
    rank            TEXT,                  -- "I", "II", "III", "IV"
    lp              INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    wins            INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    losses          INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    updated_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- Unix timestamp
    PRIMARY KEY (puuid, region)
);

-- Per-guild bot settings.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guild_settings (
    guild_id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    channel_id      INTEGER,               -- where to auto-post leaderboard
    post_interval   INTEGER DEFAULT 3600,  -- seconds between auto-posts
    last_post_at    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     -- Unix timestamp of last auto-post
    last_message_id INTEGER                -- message to edit on next auto-post
);
```

Key decisions:
- `rank_cache` is keyed by `(puuid, region)`, not by `(guild_id, discord_id)`. If the same Riot account appears across two Discord servers (unlikely but possible), they share one cache row. This is intentional — it halves API calls.
- `updated_at` is a Unix integer, not a datetime string. Simpler to compare against `time.time()` in Python.
- `linked_accounts` uses `ON DELETE CASCADE` so unregistering a player clears their linked accounts automatically.
- `guild_settings.last_message_id` enables the edit-vs-post decision for auto-posting.

---

### 3. `config.py` — Region Routing

```python
# Platform hosts (for summoner/league endpoints)
PLATFORM_HOSTS = {
    "NA": "na1.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "kr.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "euw1.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "eun1.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "br1.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "jp1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "la1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "la2.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "oc1.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "tr1.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "ru.api.riotgames.com",
    "SG": "sg2.api.riotgames.com",
    "PH": "ph2.api.riotgames.com",
    "TW": "tw2.api.riotgames.com",
    "VN": "vn2.api.riotgames.com",
    "TH": "th2.api.riotgames.com",
}

# Regional routing hosts (for account-v1 PUUID lookups)
REGIONAL_HOSTS = {
    "NA": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "SG": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "PH": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "TW": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "VN": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "TH": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "sea.api.riotgames.com",
}

TIER_ORDER = [
    "IRON", "BRONZE", "SILVER", "GOLD", "PLATINUM",
    "EMERALD", "DIAMOND", "MASTER", "GRANDMASTER", "CHALLENGER"
]
RANK_ORDER = ["IV", "III", "II", "I"]
CACHE_TTL = 300  # 5 minutes in seconds
PLAYERS_PER_PAGE = 10
```

---

### 4. `riot_client.py` — API Wrapper

A single `RiotClient` class holds one `aiohttp.ClientSession` created in an async context manager. All methods raise a custom `RiotAPIError` subclass with the HTTP status attached — callers catch it and surface a clean message to Discord.

Key methods:

```python
class RiotClient:
    async def get_puuid(self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str) -> str
    async def get_summoner_by_puuid(self, puuid: str, region: str) -> dict
    async def get_tft_rank(self, summoner_id: str, region: str) -> dict | None
    # Returns the RANKED_TFT entry dict, or None if unranked.
    # Filters internally: next(e for e in data if e["queueType"] == "RANKED_TFT", None)

    async def get_full_rank_data(self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str) -> dict
    # Convenience: chains all three calls, returns unified dict.
```

Rate limiting: Riot's development key is 20 req/1s, 100 req/2min. For a small bot (<50 players), this won't be an issue with 5-minute caching. Do not implement a rate limiter for now — handle 429 responses by catching them in `RiotAPIError` and logging. Add a backoff decorator only when you actually hit limits in production.

The session is created once in `bot.py` (`async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session`) and passed into the cog constructors. Do not create sessions per-request.

---

### 5. `cache.py` — In-Memory TTL Layer

The SQLite `rank_cache` table IS the cache (persists across restarts). The in-memory layer is optional but useful for burst deduplication within a single process. Recommended approach: skip a separate in-memory cache. Use the DB directly.

Why: aiosqlite reads from WAL-mode SQLite are fast (microseconds). The TTL check is a single `SELECT updated_at FROM rank_cache WHERE puuid=? AND region=?`. The 5-minute TTL provides sufficient protection against the Riot API rate limit. Adding an in-memory dict creates a second source of truth to keep in sync, which is a bug surface. Keep it simple.

If you later need sub-100ms response to a slash command, add:
```python
# In database.py
_mem_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[dict, float]] = {}
```
...as a module-level dict, with a helper that checks it before hitting SQLite. But do not do this at initial build.

---

### 6. Cog Design

#### `cogs/registration.py`

Commands:
- `/register <game_name> <tag_line>` — Links the invoking Discord user to an NA Riot account for this guild. Resolves puuid + summoner_id on the spot (immediate Riot API call), stores in `registrations`. Fetches and caches initial rank. Ephemeral response.
- `/unregister` — Removes the user's row from `registrations` (CASCADE deletes linked accounts). Ephemeral response.
- `/link-region <region> <game_name> <tag_line>` — Adds a row to `linked_accounts`. Resolves puuid + summoner_id for that region's platform host. Validates that `region` is in `PLATFORM_HOSTS`. Ephemeral response.
- `/unlink-region <region>` — Removes a specific `linked_accounts` row.
- `/my-accounts` — Shows the user's NA account and all linked regions. Ephemeral embed. No rank data, just account names.

Registration flow for `/register`:
1. Call `riot_client.get_puuid(game_name, tag_line, "NA")`
2. Call `riot_client.get_summoner_by_puuid(puuid, "NA")`
3. `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO registrations ...`
4. Call `riot_client.get_tft_rank(summoner_id, "NA")` and write to `rank_cache`
5. Reply with confirmation embed showing current rank

#### `cogs/leaderboard.py`

Commands:
- `/leaderboard` — Fetches all registrations for the guild, pulls rank from `rank_cache` (refreshing stale entries), sorts by rank, renders paginated embed.

The `PaginatedView` class:

```python
class PaginatedView(discord.ui.View):
    def __init__(self, pages: list[discord.Embed], timeout: float = 180):
        ...
    @discord.ui.button(label="◀", style=discord.ButtonStyle.secondary)
    async def prev_page(self, interaction, button): ...
    @discord.ui.button(label="▶", style=discord.ButtonStyle.secondary)
    async def next_page(self, interaction, button): ...
```

Each page is a pre-built `discord.Embed`. Build all pages before showing the view. Disable buttons at boundaries (first page: disable prev; last page: disable next). Update by calling `interaction.response.edit_message(embed=self.pages[self.current], view=self)`.

Rank sorting: convert `(tier, rank, lp)` to a numeric score for `sorted()`:
```python
def rank_score(row) -> int:
    if row["tier"] is None:
        return -1
    tier_val = TIER_ORDER.index(row["tier"]) * 10000
    rank_val = RANK_ORDER.index(row["rank"]) * 400 if row["rank"] else 0
    return tier_val + rank_val + row["lp"]
```

Embed format per player (one line per player in the field):
```
1. PlayerName#TAG — Diamond II 75 LP  |  [KR] GrandmasterRiotID#KR1
```
The linked region data is displayed as a parenthetical suffix. If the linked account's rank cache is stale, show `[KR] ⏳ updating...` and kick off a background task to refresh it.

#### `cogs/admin.py`

Commands:
- `/set-channel <channel>` — Writes `channel_id` to `guild_settings`. Requires `manage_guild` permission check via `@app_commands.default_permissions(manage_guild=True)`.
- `/set-interval <hours>` — Writes `post_interval` (converted to seconds) to `guild_settings`. Minimum 1 hour enforced in code.
- `/force-refresh` — Deletes all `rank_cache` rows for this guild's players and immediately re-fetches. Useful after a patch. Responds with a progress message that gets edited on completion.
- `/force-post` — Manually triggers the auto-post logic for this guild.

---

### 7. Background Task: Cache Refresh + Auto-Posting

In `bot.py`, register one `tasks.loop` task. Do not put it in a cog — lifecycle management is cleaner on the bot object itself.

```python
@tasks.loop(minutes=1)
async def background_loop(bot):
    now = int(time.time())

    # Step 1: Refresh stale rank cache entries
    stale_rows = await db.get_stale_cache_entries(now - CACHE_TTL)
    for row in stale_rows:
        try:
            rank_data = await riot_client.get_tft_rank(row["summoner_id"], row["region"])
            await db.upsert_rank_cache(row["puuid"], row["region"], rank_data, now)
        except RiotAPIError as e:
            log.warning(f"Failed to refresh {row['puuid']}/{row['region']}: {e}")

    # Step 2: Auto-post leaderboards for guilds whose interval has elapsed
    guilds_due = await db.get_guilds_due_for_post(now)
    for guild_settings in guilds_due:
        await post_leaderboard(bot, guild_settings)
        await db.update_last_post(guild_settings["guild_id"], now)
```

The `get_stale_cache_entries` query joins `rank_cache` against both `registrations` and `linked_accounts` so it only refreshes players that are still registered somewhere. This prevents accumulating orphaned cache rows.

```sql
-- get_stale_cache_entries
SELECT rc.puuid, rc.region,
       COALESCE(r.summoner_id, la.summoner_id) as summoner_id
FROM rank_cache rc
LEFT JOIN registrations r   ON r.puuid = rc.puuid AND rc.region = 'NA'
LEFT JOIN linked_accounts la ON la.puuid = rc.puuid AND la.region = rc.region
WHERE rc.updated_at < :cutoff
  AND (r.puuid IS NOT NULL OR la.puuid IS NOT NULL)
```

The loop runs every 1 minute but only acts when TTL or interval conditions are met. This gives you fine-grained auto-post scheduling without needing per-guild async tasks.

---

### 8. Auto-Posting: Edit vs. New Message

Strategy: **always try to edit; fall back to a new post.**

```python
async def post_leaderboard(bot, guild_settings):
    channel = bot.get_channel(guild_settings["channel_id"])
    if channel is None:
        return

    embeds = await build_leaderboard_embeds(guild_settings["guild_id"])
    # Auto-posts always show page 1 only, no pagination buttons
    # (pagination is only for interactive /leaderboard responses)
    embed = embeds[0]
    embed.set_footer(text=f"Page 1/{len(embeds)} • Updated {datetime.utcnow():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M} UTC")

    if guild_settings["last_message_id"]:
        try:
            msg = await channel.fetch_message(guild_settings["last_message_id"])
            await msg.edit(embed=embed)
            return
        except (discord.NotFound, discord.Forbidden):
            pass  # fall through to posting new

    msg = await channel.send(embed=embed)
    await db.update_last_message_id(guild_settings["guild_id"], msg.id)
```

Why page 1 only for auto-posts: auto-posted messages are passive — they sit in a channel as a status board. Interactive pagination belongs to slash command responses which have a 15-minute interaction token window anyway. If the leaderboard has multiple pages, indicate it in the footer. Users who want to paginate use `/leaderboard`.

---

### 9. `bot.py` — Entry Point Structure

```python
class TFTBot(commands.Bot):
    def __init__(self):
        intents = discord.Intents.default()
        # No message_content intent needed — slash commands only
        super().__init__(command_prefix=None, intents=intents)
        self.db: Database = None
        self.riot: RiotClient = None

    async def setup_hook(self):
        self.db = Database("tft.db")
        await self.db.init()  # runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
        self.riot = RiotClient(session=aiohttp.ClientSession(),
                               api_key=os.environ["RIOT_API_KEY"])
        await self.load_extension("cogs.registration")
        await self.load_extension("cogs.leaderboard")
        await self.load_extension("cogs.admin")
        await self.tree.sync()  # sync slash commands globally
        background_loop.start(self)

    async def close(self):
        background_loop.cancel()
        await self.riot.session.close()
        await self.db.close()
        await super().close()
```

`setup_hook` is the correct discord.py 2.x lifecycle method for async initialization. Do not use `on_ready` for setup — it can fire multiple times.

Call `tree.sync()` globally on every startup during development. Once the command list is stable, comment it out (global sync propagates in ~1 hour but isn't rate-limited; guild sync is instant but per-guild).

---

### 10. `requirements.txt`

```
discord.py>=2.3.0
aiosqlite>=0.19.0
aiohttp>=3.9.0
python-dotenv>=1.0.0
```

`.env` file:
```
DISCORD_TOKEN=your_token_here
RIOT_API_KEY=RGAPI-xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

---

### 11. Architectural Decisions Summary

**SQLite over PostgreSQL**: For a single-process bot tracking under a few hundred players, SQLite with WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`) is sufficient and eliminates an external dependency. Switch to PostgreSQL if you need multi-process deployment or hit WAL contention.

**One aiohttp session for all Riot calls**: Sessions are expensive to create. Create one in `setup_hook`, pass it to `RiotClient`, close it in `bot.close()`.

**Cogs receive `db` and `riot` via constructor injection**: The cog `__init__` receives the already-initialized `Database` and `RiotClient` objects from the bot. Avoids globals or `self.bot.db` access patterns that are hard to test.

**No separate in-memory cache**: SQLite WAL reads are fast enough. The DB is the single source of truth. Revisit after profiling if `/leaderboard` response time exceeds 2 seconds.

**puuid as the stable identifier**: Riot IDs (gameName#tagLine) can change. puuid is permanent. Store puuid in all tables; only store the display gameName+tagLine for rendering.

**Per-guild isolation via `guild_id` in every query**: All data is scoped to `guild_id`. The bot can be in 100 servers with no data leakage between them.

**Refresh stale entries proactively in the background**: The 1-minute background loop ensures cache entries are refreshed before users ask for the leaderboard, not on-demand. This means `/leaderboard` responses are nearly instantaneous — they read from cache and only block if an entry is stale AND hasn't been refreshed yet (race condition window: at most 1 minute).

**`/register` resolves and caches immediately**: Registration is the one place where a blocking Riot API call is acceptable — the user just submitted a command and is waiting for confirmation. All subsequent data is served from cache.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/bot.py` — Entry point, bot class, background loop, lifecycle management
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/database.py` — Schema, all SQL helpers, aiosqlite connection management
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/riot_client.py` — All Riot API HTTP logic, region routing, error handling
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/cogs/leaderboard.py` — Rank sorting, embed building, PaginatedView, auto-post render
- `/Users/ag/codin/tft-bot-claude/config.py` — Region maps, TIER_ORDER, RANK_ORDER, constants shared across all modules

import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

DISCORD_TOKEN: str = os.environ["DISCORD_TOKEN"]
RIOT_API_KEY: str = os.environ["RIOT_API_KEY"]
DB_PATH: str = os.getenv("DB_PATH", "tft.db")
CACHE_TTL: int = int(os.getenv("CACHE_TTL", "300"))  # seconds; default 5 minutes

PLAYERS_PER_PAGE = 10

# Maps our region key → Riot platform host (summoner/league endpoints)
PLATFORM_HOSTS: dict[str, str] = {
    "NA": "na1.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "kr.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "euw1.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "eun1.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "br1.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "jp1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "la1.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "la2.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "oc1.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "tr1.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "ru.api.riotgames.com",
}

# Maps our region key → Riot regional routing host (account-v1 / PUUID lookup)
REGIONAL_HOSTS: dict[str, str] = {
    "NA": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "BR": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAN": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "LAS": "americas.api.riotgames.com",
    "KR": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "JP": "asia.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUW": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "EUNE": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "TR": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "RU": "europe.api.riotgames.com",
    "OCE": "sea.api.riotgames.com",
}

VALID_REGIONS = list(PLATFORM_HOSTS.keys())

# Ordered lowest → highest for scoring
TIER_ORDER: list[str] = [
    "IRON", "BRONZE", "SILVER", "GOLD", "PLATINUM",
    "EMERALD", "DIAMOND", "MASTER", "GRANDMASTER", "CHALLENGER",
]
RANK_ORDER: list[str] = ["IV", "III", "II", "I"]

TIER_EMOJIS: dict[str, str] = {
    "IRON": "⬛",
    "BRONZE": "🟫",
    "SILVER": "⬜",
    "GOLD": "🟨",
    "PLATINUM": "🩵",
    "EMERALD": "💚",
    "DIAMOND": "💙",
    "MASTER": "🟣",
    "GRANDMASTER": "🟥",
    "CHALLENGER": "⭐",
}

# Embed accent color per top-ranked tier
TIER_COLORS: dict[str, int] = {
    "IRON": 0x8B8B8B,
    "BRONZE": 0xCD7F32,
    "SILVER": 0xC0C0C0,
    "GOLD": 0xFFD700,
    "PLATINUM": 0x00B4D8,
    "EMERALD": 0x50C878,
    "DIAMOND": 0xB9F2FF,
    "MASTER": 0x9932CC,
    "GRANDMASTER": 0xFF4500,
    "CHALLENGER": 0xFFD700,
}


def rank_score(tier: str | None, division: str | None, lp: int) -> int:
    """Return a numeric score for sorting; unranked players score -1."""
    if not tier:
        return -1
    tier_val = TIER_ORDER.index(tier) if tier in TIER_ORDER else -1
    div_val = RANK_ORDER.index(division) if division in RANK_ORDER else 0
    return tier_val * 10_000 + div_val * 400 + (lp or 0)


def format_rank(tier: str | None, division: str | None, lp: int) -> str:
    """Return a human-readable rank string."""
    if not tier:
        return "Unranked"
    emoji = TIER_EMOJIS.get(tier, "")
    tier_str = tier.title()
    if tier in ("MASTER", "GRANDMASTER", "CHALLENGER"):
        return f"{emoji} {tier_str} {lp:,} LP"
    return f"{emoji} {tier_str} {division} {lp:,} LP"


MEDALS: dict[int, str] = {1: "🥇", 2: "🥈", 3: "🥉"}

```python
import time
from typing import Any

import aiosqlite

from config import DB_PATH


class Database:
    def __init__(self, path: str = DB_PATH):
        self.path = path
        self._db: aiosqlite.Connection | None = None

    async def init(self) -> None:
        self._db = await aiosqlite.connect(self.path)
        self._db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row
        await self._db.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
        await self._db.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
        await self._create_tables()

    async def close(self) -> None:
        if self._db:
            await self._db.close()
            self._db = None

    async def _create_tables(self) -> None:
        await self._db.executescript("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS registrations (
                guild_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
                discord_id     INTEGER NOT NULL,
                game_name      TEXT    NOT NULL,
                tag_line       TEXT    NOT NULL,
                puuid          TEXT    NOT NULL,
                summoner_id    TEXT    NOT NULL,
                PRIMARY KEY (guild_id, discord_id)
            );

            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS linked_accounts (
                id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                guild_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
                discord_id     INTEGER NOT NULL,
                region         TEXT    NOT NULL,
                game_name      TEXT    NOT NULL,
                tag_line       TEXT    NOT NULL,
                puuid          TEXT    NOT NULL,
                summoner_id    TEXT    NOT NULL,
                UNIQUE (guild_id, discord_id, region),
                FOREIGN KEY (guild_id, discord_id)
                    REFERENCES registrations(guild_id, discord_id)
                    ON DELETE CASCADE
            );

            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rank_cache (
                puuid          TEXT    NOT NULL,
                region         TEXT    NOT NULL,
                tier           TEXT,
                division       TEXT,
                lp             INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                wins           INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                losses         INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                updated_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,
                PRIMARY KEY (puuid, region)
            );

            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guild_settings (
                guild_id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                channel_id          INTEGER,
                post_interval       INTEGER DEFAULT 3600,
                last_post_at        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                last_message_id     INTEGER
            );
        """)
        await self._db.commit()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Registrations                                                         #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    async def upsert_registration(
        self,
        guild_id: int,
        discord_id: int,
        game_name: str,
        tag_line: str,
        puuid: str,
        summoner_id: str,
    ) -> None:
        await self._db.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO registrations (guild_id, discord_id, game_name, tag_line, puuid, summoner_id)
            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            ON CONFLICT(guild_id, discord_id) DO UPDATE SET
                game_name   = excluded.game_name,
                tag_line    = excluded.tag_line,
                puuid       = excluded.puuid,
                summoner_id = excluded.summoner_id
            """,
            (guild_id, discord_id, game_name, tag_line, puuid, summoner_id),
        )
        await self._db.commit()

    async def delete_registration(self, guild_id: int, discord_id: int) -> bool:
        cursor = await self._db.execute(
            "DELETE FROM registrations WHERE guild_id = ? AND discord_id = ?",
            (guild_id, discord_id),
        )
        await self._db.commit()
        return cursor.rowcount > 0

    async def get_registration(
        self, guild_id: int, discord_id: int
    ) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        async with self._db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM registrations WHERE guild_id = ? AND discord_id = ?",
            (guild_id, discord_id),
        ) as cur:
            row = await cur.fetchone()
            return dict(row) if row else None

    async def get_all_registrations(self, guild_id: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        async with self._db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM registrations WHERE guild_id = ?", (guild_id,)
        ) as cur:
            rows = await cur.fetchall()
            return [dict(r) for r in rows]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Linked accounts                                                       #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    async def upsert_linked_account(
        self,
        guild_id: int,
        discord_id: int,
        region: str,
        game_name: str,
        tag_line: str,
        puuid: str,
        summoner_id: str,
    ) -> None:
        await self._db.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO linked_accounts
                (guild_id, discord_id, region, game_name, tag_line, puuid, summoner_id)
            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            ON CONFLICT(guild_id, discord_id, region) DO UPDATE SET
                game_name   = excluded.game_name,
                tag_line    = excluded.tag_line,
                puuid       = excluded.puuid,
                summoner_id = excluded.summoner_id
            """,
            (guild_id, discord_id, region, game_name, tag_line, puuid, summoner_id),
        )
        await self._db.commit()

    async def delete_linked_account(
        self, guild_id: int, discord_id: int, region: str
    ) -> bool:
        cursor = await self._db.execute(
            "DELETE FROM linked_accounts WHERE guild_id = ? AND discord_id = ? AND region = ?",
            (guild_id, discord_id, region),
        )
        await self._db.commit()
        return cursor.rowcount > 0

    async def get_linked_accounts(
        self, guild_id: int, discord_id: int
    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        async with self._db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM linked_accounts WHERE guild_id = ? AND discord_id = ?",
            (guild_id, discord_id),
        ) as cur:
            rows = await cur.fetchall()
            return [dict(r) for r in rows]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Rank cache                                                            #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    async def upsert_rank_cache(
        self,
        puuid: str,
        region: str,
        tier: str | None,
        division: str | None,
        lp: int,
        wins: int,
        losses: int,
    ) -> None:
        await self._db.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO rank_cache (puuid, region, tier, division, lp, wins, losses, updated_at)
            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            ON CONFLICT(puuid, region) DO UPDATE SET
                tier       = excluded.tier,
                division   = excluded.division,
                lp         = excluded.lp,
                wins       = excluded.wins,
                losses     = excluded.losses,
                updated_at = excluded.updated_at
            """,
            (puuid, region, tier, division, lp, wins, losses, int(time.time())),
        )
        await self._db.commit()

    async def get_rank_cache(
        self, puuid: str, region: str
    ) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        async with self._db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM rank_cache WHERE puuid = ? AND region = ?",
            (puuid, region),
        ) as cur:
            row = await cur.fetchone()
            return dict(row) if row else None

    async def get_stale_cache_entries(self, cutoff: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Return rank_cache rows older than cutoff that still belong to an active player."""
        async with self._db.execute(
            """
            SELECT rc.puuid, rc.region,
                   COALESCE(r.summoner_id, la.summoner_id) AS summoner_id
            FROM rank_cache rc
            LEFT JOIN registrations  r  ON r.puuid  = rc.puuid AND rc.region = 'NA'
            LEFT JOIN linked_accounts la ON la.puuid = rc.puuid AND la.region = rc.region
            WHERE rc.updated_at < ?
              AND (r.puuid IS NOT NULL OR la.puuid IS NOT NULL)
            """,
            (cutoff,),
        ) as cur:
            rows = await cur.fetchall()
            return [dict(r) for r in rows]

    async def invalidate_guild_cache(self, guild_id: int) -> None:
        """Set all cache entries for a guild's players to expired (updated_at = 0)."""
        await self._db.execute(
            """
            UPDATE rank_cache SET updated_at = 0
            WHERE puuid IN (SELECT puuid FROM registrations WHERE guild_id = ?)
               OR puuid IN (SELECT puuid FROM linked_accounts WHERE guild_id = ?)
            """,
            (guild_id, guild_id),
        )
        await self._db.commit()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Guild settings                                                        #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    async def get_guild_settings(self, guild_id: int) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        async with self._db.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM guild_settings WHERE guild_id = ?", (guild_id,)
        ) as cur:
            row = await cur.fetchone()
            return dict(row) if row else None

    async def upsert_guild_settings(self, guild_id: int, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        existing = await self.get_guild_settings(guild_id)
        if existing:
            if not kwargs:
                return
            set_clause = ", ".join(f"{k} = ?" for k in kwargs)
            await self._db.execute(
                f"UPDATE guild_settings SET {set_clause} WHERE guild_id = ?",
                [*kwargs.values(), guild_id],
            )
        else:
            kwargs["guild_id"] = guild_id
            cols = ", ".join(kwargs.keys())
            placeholders = ", ".join("?" * len(kwargs))
            await self._db.execute(
                f"INSERT INTO guild_settings ({cols}) VALUES ({placeholders})",
                list(kwargs.values()),
            )
        await self._db.commit()

    async def get_guilds_due_for_post(self, now: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Return guild settings rows where auto-post is configured and interval has elapsed."""
        async with self._db.execute(
            """
            SELECT * FROM guild_settings
            WHERE channel_id IS NOT NULL
              AND post_interval > 0
              AND (? - last_post_at) >= post_interval
            """,
            (now,),
        ) as cur:
            rows = await cur.fetchall()
            return [dict(r) for r in rows]

```

```python
import asyncio
import logging
from typing import Any

import aiohttp

from config import PLATFORM_HOSTS, REGIONAL_HOSTS

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class RiotAPIError(Exception):
    def __init__(self, status: int, message: str):
        self.status = status
        self.message = message
        super().__init__(f"Riot API {status}: {message}")


class NotFoundError(RiotAPIError):
    pass


class RateLimitError(RiotAPIError):
    def __init__(self, retry_after: int = 5):
        self.retry_after = retry_after
        super().__init__(429, f"Rate limited; retry after {retry_after}s")


class RiotClient:
    def __init__(self, session: aiohttp.ClientSession, api_key: str):
        self._session = session
        self._api_key = api_key
        # Semaphore prevents more than 5 simultaneous outbound requests
        self._sem = asyncio.Semaphore(5)

    async def _get(self, url: str) -> Any:
        headers = {"X-Riot-Token": self._api_key}
        async with self._sem:
            async with self._session.get(url, headers=headers) as resp:
                if resp.status == 200:
                    return await resp.json()
                if resp.status == 404:
                    raise NotFoundError(404, "Not found")
                if resp.status == 429:
                    retry_after = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "5"))
                    raise RateLimitError(retry_after)
                if resp.status == 403:
                    raise RiotAPIError(403, "API key forbidden — check your Riot developer key")
                if resp.status == 401:
                    raise RiotAPIError(401, "API key unauthorized — key may be expired")
                text = await resp.text()
                raise RiotAPIError(resp.status, text[:200])

    async def get_account_by_riot_id(
        self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str = "NA"
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Resolves gameName + tagLine → account data including puuid.
        Uses the regional routing host (americas/asia/europe/sea).
        """
        regional = REGIONAL_HOSTS.get(region.upper(), "americas.api.riotgames.com")
        url = (
            f"https://{regional}/riot/account/v1/accounts/by-riot-id"
            f"/{game_name}/{tag_line}"
        )
        return await self._get(url)

    async def get_tft_summoner_by_puuid(
        self, puuid: str, region: str = "NA"
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Resolves puuid → TFT summoner data (includes encrypted summoner id)."""
        platform = PLATFORM_HOSTS.get(region.upper(), "na1.api.riotgames.com")
        url = f"https://{platform}/tft/summoner/v1/summoners/by-puuid/{puuid}"
        return await self._get(url)

    async def get_tft_rank(
        self, summoner_id: str, region: str = "NA"
    ) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        """
        Returns the RANKED_TFT league entry for a summoner, or None if unranked.
        """
        platform = PLATFORM_HOSTS.get(region.upper(), "na1.api.riotgames.com")
        url = f"https://{platform}/tft/league/v1/entries/by-summoner/{summoner_id}"
        entries: list[dict] = await self._get(url)
        return next(
            (e for e in entries if e.get("queueType") == "RANKED_TFT"), None
        )

    async def resolve_player(
        self, game_name: str, tag_line: str, region: str = "NA"
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Full resolution: gameName#tagLine → {puuid, summoner_id, tier, division, lp, wins, losses}.
        Raises RiotAPIError on any failure.
        """
        account = await self.get_account_by_riot_id(game_name, tag_line, region)
        puuid: str = account["puuid"]
        # Use the canonical casing returned by Riot
        game_name = account["gameName"]
        tag_line = account["tagLine"]

        summoner = await self.get_tft_summoner_by_puuid(puuid, region)
        summoner_id: str = summoner["id"]

        rank_entry = await self.get_tft_rank(summoner_id, region)

        return {
            "puuid": puuid,
            "summoner_id": summoner_id,
            "game_name": game_name,
            "tag_line": tag_line,
            "tier": rank_entry["tier"] if rank_entry else None,
            "division": rank_entry["rank"] if rank_entry else None,
            "lp": rank_entry["leaguePoints"] if rank_entry else 0,
            "wins": rank_entry["wins"] if rank_entry else 0,
            "losses": rank_entry["losses"] if rank_entry else 0,
        }

    async def refresh_rank(
        self, summoner_id: str, region: str = "NA"
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Fetch only the rank for an already-resolved summoner.
        Returns dict with tier/division/lp/wins/losses (tier is None if unranked).
        """
        rank_entry = await self.get_tft_rank(summoner_id, region)
        return {
            "tier": rank_entry["tier"] if rank_entry else None,
            "division": rank_entry["rank"] if rank_entry else None,
            "lp": rank_entry["leaguePoints"] if rank_entry else 0,
            "wins": rank_entry["wins"] if rank_entry else 0,
            "losses": rank_entry["losses"] if rank_entry else 0,
        }

import logging

import discord
from discord import app_commands
from discord.ext import commands

from config import VALID_REGIONS, format_rank, TIER_COLORS
from riot_client import NotFoundError, RiotAPIError

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class Registration(commands.Cog):
    def __init__(self, bot: commands.Bot):
        self.bot = bot

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # /register                                                             #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @app_commands.command(
        name="register",
        description="Register your NA TFT account to this server's leaderboard.",
    )
    @app_commands.describe(
        game_name="Your Riot Games username (the part before #)",
        tag_line="Your Riot tag (the part after #, e.g. NA1)",
    )
    async def register(
        self,
        interaction: discord.Interaction,
        game_name: str,
        tag_line: str,
    ) -> None:
        await interaction.response.defer(ephemeral=True)

        try:
            data = await self.bot.riot.resolve_player(game_name, tag_line, region="NA")
        except NotFoundError:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                f"Could not find Riot account **{game_name}#{tag_line}**. "
                "Double-check your username and tag.",
                ephemeral=True,
            )
            return
        except RiotAPIError as e:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                f"Riot API error: {e.message}", ephemeral=True
            )
            return

        await self.bot.db.upsert_registration(
            guild_id=interaction.guild_id,
            discord_id=interaction.user.id,
            game_name=data["game_name"],
            tag_line=data["tag_line"],
            puuid=data["puuid"],
            summoner_id=data["summoner_id"],
        )
        await self.bot.db.upsert_rank_cache(
            puuid=data["puuid"],
            region="NA",
            tier=data["tier"],
            division=data["division"],
            lp=data["lp"],
            wins=data["wins"],
            losses=data["losses"],
        )

        rank_str = format_rank(data["tier"], data["division"], data["lp"])
        color = TIER_COLORS.get(data["tier"] or "", 0x2B2D31)
        embed = discord.Embed(
            title="✅ Registered!",
            description=(
                f"**{data['game_name']}#{data['tag_line']}** has been added to "
                f"**{interaction.guild.name}**'s TFT leaderboard."
            ),
            color=color,
        )
        embed.add_field(name="Current Rank", value=rank_str, inline=True)
        if data["tier"]:
            total = (data["wins"] or 0) + (data["losses"] or 0)
            if total:
                wr = round(data["wins"] / total * 100, 1)
                embed.add_field(
                    name="Win Rate",
                    value=f"{wr}% ({data['wins']}W / {data['losses']}L)",
                    inline=True,
                )
        embed.set_footer(text="Use /link-region to link your main account in another region.")
        await interaction.followup.send(embed=embed, ephemeral=True)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # /unregister                                                           #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @app_commands.command(
        name="unregister",
        description="Remove yourself from this server's TFT leaderboard.",
    )
    async def unregister(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
        removed = await self.bot.db.delete_registration(
            interaction.guild_id, interaction.user.id
        )
        if removed:
            await interaction.response.send_message(
                "You have been removed from this server's leaderboard.", ephemeral=True
            )
        else:
            await interaction.response.send_message(
                "You are not registered on this server's leaderboard.", ephemeral=True
            )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # /link-region                                                          #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @app_commands.command(
        name="link-region",
        description="Link a TFT account in another region to display alongside your NA rank.",
    )
    @app_commands.describe(
        region="The region of the account you want to link (e.g. KR, EUW, EUNE)",
        game_name="Riot username for that account (part before #)",
        tag_line="Riot tag for that account (part after #)",
    )
    @app_commands.choices(
        region=[app_commands.Choice(name=r, value=r) for r in VALID_REGIONS if r != "NA"]
    )
    async def link_region(
        self,
        interaction: discord.Interaction,
        region: str,
        game_name: str,
        tag_line: str,
    ) -> None:
        await interaction.response.defer(ephemeral=True)

        # Must be registered on this server first
        reg = await self.bot.db.get_registration(
            interaction.guild_id, interaction.user.id
        )
        if not reg:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                "You need to `/register` your NA account before linking other regions.",
                ephemeral=True,
            )
            return

        try:
            data = await self.bot.riot.resolve_player(game_name, tag_line, region=region)
        except NotFoundError:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                f"Could not find account **{game_name}#{tag_line}** in **{region}**.",
                ephemeral=True,
            )
            return
        except RiotAPIError as e:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                f"Riot API error: {e.message}", ephemeral=True
            )
            return

        await self.bot.db.upsert_linked_account(
            guild_id=interaction.guild_id,
            discord_id=interaction.user.id,
            region=region,
            game_name=data["game_name"],
            tag_line=data["tag_line"],
            puuid=data["puuid"],
            summoner_id=data["summoner_id"],
        )
        await self.bot.db.upsert_rank_cache(
            puuid=data["puuid"],
            region=region,
            tier=data["tier"],
            division=data["division"],
            lp=data["lp"],
            wins=data["wins"],
            losses=data["losses"],
        )

        rank_str = format_rank(data["tier"], data["division"], data["lp"])
        embed = discord.Embed(
            title=f"✅ {region} Account Linked",
            description=(
                f"**{data['game_name']}#{data['tag_line']}** ({region}) will appear "
                "alongside your NA rank on the leaderboard."
            ),
            color=TIER_COLORS.get(data["tier"] or "", 0x2B2D31),
        )
        embed.add_field(name=f"{region} Rank", value=rank_str, inline=True)
        await interaction.followup.send(embed=embed, ephemeral=True)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # /unlink-region                                                        #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @app_commands.command(
        name="unlink-region",
        description="Remove a linked account in another region.",
    )
    @app_commands.describe(region="The region to unlink")
    @app_commands.choices(
        region=[app_commands.Choice(name=r, value=r) for r in VALID_REGIONS if r != "NA"]
    )
    async def unlink_region(
        self, interaction: discord.Interaction, region: str
    ) -> None:
        removed = await self.bot.db.delete_linked_account(
            interaction.guild_id, interaction.user.id, region
        )
        if removed:
            await interaction.response.send_message(
                f"Your **{region}** linked account has been removed.", ephemeral=True
            )
        else:
            await interaction.response.send_message(
                f"You don't have a **{region}** account linked.", ephemeral=True
            )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # /my-accounts                                                          #
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #

    @app_commands.command(
        name="my-accounts",
        description="View your registered NA account and all linked regional accounts.",
    )
    async def my_accounts(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
        await interaction.response.defer(ephemeral=True)

        reg = await self.bot.db.get_registration(
            interaction.guild_id, interaction.user.id
        )
        if not reg:
            await interaction.followup.send(
                "You are not registered. Use `/register` to add your NA account.",
                ephemeral=True,
            )
            return

        # Fetch NA rank from cache
        na_cache = await self.bot.db.get_rank_cache(reg["puuid"], "NA")
        na_rank = (
            format_rank(
                na_cache["tier"], na_cache["division"], na_cache["lp"]
            )
            if na_cache
            else "Unranked"
        )

        embed = discord.Embed(
            title=f"Accounts for {interaction.user.display_name}",
            color=TIER_COLORS.get(
                (na_cache or {}).get("tier") or "", 0x2B2D31
            ),
        )
        embed.add_field(
            name="🇺🇸 NA (Primary)",
            value=f"**{reg['game_name']}#{reg['tag_line']}**\n{na_rank}",
            inline=False,
        )

        linked = await self.bot.db.get_linked_accounts(
            interaction.guild_id, interaction.user.id
        )
        for acct in linked:
            cache = await self.bot.db.get_rank_cache(acct["puuid"], acct["region"])
            rank_str = (
                format_rank(cache["tier"], cache["division"], cache["lp"])
                if cache
                else "Unranked"
            )
            embed.add_field(
                name=f"🌐 {acct['region']} (Linked)",
                value=f"**{acct['game_name']}#{acct['tag_line']}**\n{rank_str}",
                inline=False,
            )

        if not linked:
            embed.set_footer(
                text="Use /link-region to add accounts in other regions."
            )
        await interaction.followup.send(embed=embed, ephemeral=True)


async def setup(bot: commands.Bot) -> None:
    await bot.add_cog(Registration(bot))
